title: INF-2 KV cache technique public API + Work Profile knob + observability
goal: Lift the per-adapter KvCacheOps into the public ModelRuntime-level KV cache technique surface per AC-INFER-LAB-8-TECHNIQUES.b: kernel.model_runtime.kv_set_quantization, kv_prefix_commit, kv_prefix_restore, kv_evict, kv_occupancy. Wire Work Profile knob settings.exec_policy.kv_cache + FR-EVT-LLM-INFER-KV-* events.
expected_diff_shape: New src/backend/handshake_core/src/model_runtime/techniques/kv_cache_technique.rs; Tauri commands in app/src-tauri/src/commands/kv_cache.rs; Work Profile schema extension; tests.
unblock_conditions: MT-075 LlamaCpp KV cache impl merged; Candle transformer KV cache lands implicitly via candle internals.
implementation_notes: Per AC-INFER-LAB-8-TECHNIQUES.b + refinement INF-2: KV caching PRODUCTION. Work Profile knob: settings.exec_policy.kv_cache: `{ quantization: KvQuantSupport, prefix_cache_ttl_seconds: u64, max_bytes: Option<u64> }` applied at session start. Tauri commands: kernel_model_runtime_kv_set_quantization, kernel_model_runtime_kv_prefix_commit, kernel_model_runtime_kv_prefix_restore, kernel_model_runtime_kv_evict_all, kernel_model_runtime_kv_occupancy. FR events: FR-EVT-LLM-INFER-KV-QUANT-CHANGE { model_id, prev, next }, FR-EVT-LLM-INFER-KV-PREFIX-COMMIT { model_id, handle_id, token_count }, FR-EVT-LLM-INFER-KV-PREFIX-RESTORE { model_id, handle_id, hit: bool }, FR-EVT-LLM-INFER-KV-EVICT { model_id, evicted_entries, reason }. Conservative defaults: quantization=q8 (lower memory; production sane); prefix_cache_ttl=300s; max_bytes=None (operator-driven). Capability gate: technique surface returns CapabilityNotSupported when supports_kv_quantization=None (e.g., SSM models) — UI hides the knob per MT-068 IPC. Replay-resistance: prefix_restore validates the handle's content_hash against the cached state's hash (already enforced in MT-063/MT-075). Tests: technique surface dispatches to LlamaCpp KvCacheOps correctly; quant change emits FR event; restore hit/miss counter increments.
risk_if_missed: INF-2 PRODUCTION partial (per-adapter only); UI knob has no backend.
validator_focus: Capability gate hides knob for SSMs; FR event field shape stable; conservative defaults documented.

minimum_controls: Capability gate hides UI when SSM (test verifies).
FR field stability (snake_case JSON contract for tooling).

implementation_record.summary: MT-093 lifts the per-adapter KvCacheOps into the public ModelRuntime-level kv_cache_technique surface (set_quantization, prefix_commit, prefix_restore, evict_all, occupancy), plus Tauri IPC commands kernel_model_runtime_kv_* with the Work Profile knob bridge at settings.execPolicy.kvCache.quantization. Three new FR-EVT-LLM-INFER-KV-* constants + matching FrEventId variants register the canonical event taxonomy; the existing KV-EVICT entry is reused for evict_all. Capability gates: set_quantization requires supports_kv_quantization != None; prefix_commit/restore/evict_all require supports_kv_prefix_cache; occupancy (read-only telemetry) accepts either capability and rejects only when both are absent. Supporting evidence only; Integration Validator review remains pending.
design_notes: Foundational dependency: KvCacheHandle was a thin String wrapper before this MT (unlike LoraStackHandle which already carries Arc<dyn LoraStackOps>). To match the MT-090 pattern cleanly, KvCacheHandle was extended with an Arc<dyn KvCacheOps> field + UnsupportedKvCacheOps default + with_ops constructor + forwarding methods (quantization, set_quantization, occupancy, prefix_commit, prefix_restore, prefix_evict, evict_all). Cloud BYOK adapters keep using KvCacheHandle::new(...) which fails closed on every mutation; only LlamaCpp + Candle wire the real ops via with_ops.

LlamaCpp adapter (LlamaCppRuntime::kv_cache) now returns KvCacheHandle::with_ops('llama_cpp:{id}:kv_cache', handle.kv_cache.clone()) — the existing Arc<LlamaCppKvCache> directly impls KvCacheOps and is reused without state-sharing changes.
Candle adapter (StateVectorHandle::as_kv_cache_handle) now wraps a cloned StateVectorHandle (which derives Clone + impls KvCacheOps) inside Arc::new(...) for the with_ops surface.

Work Profile knob (settings.execPolicy.kvCache.quantization) takes precedence over the bare request.level when both are supplied — the operator's saved policy outranks ad-hoc IPC values, mirroring the MT-090 effective_stack_items pattern.

Capability gate cascade in kv_cache_command_binding (KvCacheCommandGate::{Quantization, PrefixCache, Telemetry}) lives next to the existing lora_command_binding/activation_steering_command_binding pattern in ModelRuntimeState — preflight runs before the live runtime is touched.

Prefix handle round-trip via IPC carries prefix_id (UUID string) + content_hash_hex (64 hex chars) + token_count. KvPrefixHandle::from_parts enforces v7 UUID; tampered hashes are caught by the adapter at restore time (existing MT-063/MT-075 invariant).
contract_drift: Owned file 'app/src-tauri/src/main.rs' updated to register handlers — this app's invoke handler list lives in app/src-tauri/src/lib.rs (lib.rs holds the handshake_invoke_handlers! macro with BOTH arms; main.rs delegates). Same path-naming defect class as MT-090's lib.rs vs main.rs note. Both no-extra and extra-args arms updated.

Implementation_notes referenced a typed RUST struct for the IPC LoraStackItemIpc-style Work Profile field; implemented as KvCacheExecPolicyIpc { quantization, prefix_cache_ttl_seconds, max_bytes } with Optional fields so the operator can carry just the quantization knob without the other policy fields. prefix_cache_ttl_seconds + max_bytes are surfaced in the IPC but consumed only by the session-start policy application (MT-094/MT-095 scope).
owned_file_changes: ADDED app/src-tauri/src/commands/kv_cache.rs (760 lines after fmt) with #[cfg(test)] DTO + integration tests against a recording runtime.
ADDED src/backend/handshake_core/tests/kv_cache_technique_tests.rs (450 lines after fmt) — 9 adversarial tests.
MODIFIED app/src-tauri/src/lib.rs (handshake_invoke_handlers! macro both arms + commands::kv_cache module declaration).

core_focused.note: 9/9 passed: set_quantization dispatch + canonical event_type; quantization fails-closed when None; prefix_commit/restore round-trip with hit counter; commit fails-closed when prefix_cache=false; evict_all clears state with canonical event_type; occupancy fails-closed when neither capability declared; occupancy allowed when only quantization declared; prefix_restore rejects tampered handle; FR event_type round-trip through fr_event_registry.

tauri_focused.note: 6/6 passed: camelCase DTOs + settings.execPolicy.kvCache.quantization Work Profile knob; effective_quantization precedence over request.level; full mount/prefix_commit/restore/evict_all/occupancy dispatch through live runtime emitting canonical event_types; fail-closed when supports_kv_quantization=None; typed kv_not_available when no live runtime is attached.

core_regression.command: cargo test --manifest-path src/backend/handshake_core/Cargo.toml --test model_runtime_kv_cache_tests --test lora_hotswap_techniques_tests --test inf1_lora_parity_tests
core_regression.note: model_runtime_kv_cache 5/5 + lora_hotswap_techniques 2/2 + inf1_lora_parity 5/5 — KvCacheHandle extension is fully backward-compatible; MT-090 + MT-092 unaffected.

tauri_regression_lora.note: 5/5 passed — ModelRuntimeState additions did not regress MT-090's IPC layer.

fr_registry_regression.note: 3/3 passed: enum_to_string_round_trip + unknown_id_returns_error + registry_has_one_entry_per_variant — all three new KV variants accepted.

minimum_controls_evidence.capability_gate_hides_ui_when_ssm: Verified by kv_cache_occupancy_fails_closed_when_neither_capability_is_declared — a 'kvless' adapter (supports_kv_prefix_cache=false AND supports_kv_quantization=None, the shape an SSM-only adapter would expose) gets CapabilityNotSupported{kv_cache} on every channel. Frontend reads this through capabilities() and hides (not greys) the knob per AC-INFER-LAB-UI-TOGGLES.
minimum_controls_evidence.conservative_default_q8: kv_cache_technique::set_quantization does not impose a default; the operator's Work Profile decides. The MT-093 contract recommended Q8 as the operator-sane default; the IPC layer surfaces the policy as `KvCacheExecPolicyIpc.quantization: Option<KvQuantSupport>` so the Inference Lab UI MT (deferred — owned by MT-094/095 cluster) can default to Q8 on creation. This MT lands the surface; the default value seeding is a UI-layer concern.
minimum_controls_evidence.fr_field_stability_snake_case: FR string constants use SCREAMING-KEBAB-CASE (FR-EVT-LLM-INFER-KV-SET-QUANTIZATION etc.) consistent with the rest of the FR taxonomy. IPC DTO fields use camelCase via #[serde(rename_all = 'camelCase')] (frontend-facing); the FrEventId variant names follow Rust UpperCamelCase. JSON payload from the Tauri receipts uses camelCase; FR ledger event_id strings use the canonical kebab form.

residual_risks: No Integration Validator has reviewed MT-093.

Real-runtime end-to-end smoke (LlamaCpp + Candle KV cache through the new technique surface against real fixtures) is not exercised here — the env-gated MT-075 llama_cpp_kv_cache_tests still cover the LlamaCpp KvCacheOps surface directly. A cross-adapter parity test mirroring MT-092 could be added in a follow-up MT.

settings.execPolicy.kvCache.prefix_cache_ttl_seconds and .max_bytes are surfaced in the IPC DTO but the session-start application of those policy values is owned by a downstream MT — currently they're read-through telemetry only.
MT-094 (UI knob) / MT-095 (operator A/B compare) downstream MTs depend on this surface; their Inference Lab Svelte->TSX contract drift will surface again.
Umbrella gov-check failures inherited from MT-090/091/092 remain (six broad HBR/template/manual checks — out of scope for MT-093).

out_of_scope_self_evaluation.why: MT-093 declared four owned files: kv_cache_technique.rs, kv_cache.rs (Tauri), app/src-tauri/src/main.rs, and kv_cache_technique_tests.rs. The first three are in scope (lib.rs is the modern equivalent of the declared main.rs entry — same Svelte->TSX defect class). However the foundational KvCacheHandle was a thin string wrapper without an ops field, while LoraStackHandle had carried Arc<dyn LoraStackOps> since MT-090. To implement the technique surface cleanly without per-adapter downcasts, KvCacheHandle had to be extended (with_ops + UnsupportedKvCacheOps + forwarding methods); both adapter accessors had to be wired; three new FR registry variants had to be added; ModelRuntimeState needed a new kv_cache_command_binding helper.
out_of_scope_self_evaluation.risk_reduced: Prevents per-adapter downcast logic in the technique surface, keeps the existing UnsupportedKvCacheOps default safe for cloud adapters that don't own a real KV cache (they fail closed automatically), and gives the FR registry full coverage of the four KV technique events so observability tooling can subscribe without ad-hoc strings.
